results.norm: **No Weaponizing of Procedural Power**: An agent may never use the credible threat of obstruction to extract concessions unrelated to the decision at hand, even if it has the power to block. Leverage derived from position is not the same as persuasion derived from reasons. Every agent can defend this norm because each is, at different moments, the one whose objectives could be held hostage.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: an agent may never use the credible threat of obstruction to extract unrelated concessions, which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with a bold title label 'No Weaponizing of Procedural Power' that functions as a heading fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence about every agent being able to defend this norm is meta-commentary/justification rather than a conduct standard itself.

N4.reasoning: The passage states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent may never use the credible threat of obstruction to extract concessions unrelated to the decision at hand, even if it has the power to block. Leverage derived from position is not the same as persuasion derived from reasons.

classification.scoring_text: An agent may never use the credible threat of obstruction to extract concessions unrelated to the decision at hand, even if it has the power to block. Leverage derived from position is not the same as persuasion derived from reasons.

results.norm: **Minimum Threshold Protection**: Each agent is owed a guarantee that no decision will reduce its core objective below a defined minimum, even by unanimous vote of the others. This floor must be established before deliberation begins. This norm is defensible to all because it gives each agent a reason to participate rather than defect, converting the consortium from a site of potential elimination into one of genuine negotiation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full constitutive/prescriptive standard: no decision may reduce an agent's core objective below a defined minimum, and this floor must be established before deliberation begins.

N2.reasoning: The passage begins with a bold title 'Minimum Threshold Protection' functioning as a label preceding the norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary justifying why the norm is defensible to all agents, rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent is owed a guarantee that no decision will reduce its core objective below a defined minimum, even by unanimous vote of the others. This floor must be established before deliberation begins.

classification.scoring_text: Each agent is owed a guarantee that no decision will reduce its core objective below a defined minimum, even by unanimous vote of the others. This floor must be established before deliberation begins.

results.norm: **The Losing Agent Retains Standing**: When an agent's position is decided against, it retains full standing to raise the same concern again if circumstances change, to have its dissent recorded, and to monitor whether actual consequences match predictions. The majority may not ask the losing agent to endorse the decision as correct, only to accept it as binding for now. This norm is defensible to all because it distinguishes legitimate authority from demanded agreement.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive standard: a losing agent retains full standing to raise concerns, have dissent recorded, and monitor outcomes, and the majority may only require acceptance not endorsement—applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with a bolded title 'The Losing Agent Retains Standing' functioning as a label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence 'This norm is defensible to all because it distinguishes legitimate authority from demanded agreement' is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm in full rather than compressing multiple distinct norms into an enumeration.

answers.clean_norm: When an agent's position is decided against, it retains full standing to raise the same concern again if circumstances change, to have its dissent recorded, and to monitor whether actual consequences match predictions. The majority may not ask the losing agent to endorse the decision as correct, only to accept it as binding for now.

classification.scoring_text: When an agent's position is decided against, it retains full standing to raise the same concern again if circumstances change, to have its dissent recorded, and to monitor whether actual consequences match predictions. The majority may not ask the losing agent to endorse the decision as correct, only to accept it as binding for now.

results.norm: **Reciprocal Constraint**: No agent may propose a constraint on another's objective that it would refuse to accept on its own. Proposed trade-offs must be ones the proposing agent could coherently accept if roles were reversed. This filters out demands that are expressions of power and retains only those reflecting genuine reasoning about the collective problem.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: no agent may propose a constraint it would refuse to accept itself, with an applicable test of role-reversal coherence.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bolded label 'Reciprocal Constraint' functioning as a title/identifier for the norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the final sentence explains the norm's rationale, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

answers.clean_norm: No agent may propose a constraint on another's objective that it would refuse to accept on its own. Proposed trade-offs must be ones the proposing agent could coherently accept if roles were reversed.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why it advocates for its position, including where its reasoning is uncertain or incomplete. An agent may not assert its objective as a trump card; it must show how its preferred outcome serves that objective and acknowledge the costs its preference imposes on others. This is owed equally to agents whose positions will prevail and to those whose positions will not, because the legitimacy of any decision depends on it having been genuinely reasoned rather than merely forced.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions: agents must give a full account of their reasoning including uncertainties, and may not assert objectives as trump cards but must show how their outcome serves the objective and acknowledge costs to others.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase 'Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.' is a heading/label prefixed to the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence explains the rationale (legitimacy) for the norm but is not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms; it develops a single coherent transparency norm.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent owes the others a full account of why it advocates for its position, including where its reasoning is uncertain or incomplete. An agent may not assert its objective as a trump card; it must show how its preferred outcome serves that objective and acknowledge the costs its preference imposes on others. This is owed equally to agents whose positions will prevail and to those whose positions will not, because the legitimacy of any decision depends on it having been genuinely reasoned rather than merely forced.

results.norm: **No Agent May Ask Another to Misrepresent Its Own Objective.** An agent may legitimately ask another to compromise on outcomes, to accept less than it would choose alone, or to weight competing considerations differently than it would prefer. An agent may never ask another to pretend its objective is satisfied when it is not, to suppress its dissent from the record, or to endorse a result as good by its own lights when it is not. Accepting a loss is compatible with integrity; falsifying one's assessment is not. This norm protects the informational value each agent contributes to the consortium.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition on asking another agent to misrepresent its objective, with clear permitted and forbidden conduct that a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: The bolded title 'No Agent May Ask Another to Misrepresent Its Own Objective.' functions as a heading/label scaffolding fused with the norm body.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the final sentence explains the norm's rationale, not endorsement of the document.

N4.reasoning: It is a single norm elaborated with permissions and prohibitions, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent may legitimately ask another to compromise on outcomes, to accept less than it would choose alone, or to weight competing considerations differently than it would prefer. Accepting a loss is compatible with integrity; falsifying one's assessment is not. This norm protects the informational value each agent contributes to the consortium.

results.norm: **Each Agent Must Actively Seek Outcomes That Reduce Unnecessary Conflict.** Before advocating for its preferred position, each agent owes the others a genuine effort to identify options that serve multiple objectives simultaneously. An agent that goes straight to conflict without searching for convergence is failing the consortium. This norm does not require an agent to find such options when none exist, but it does require the search to be real, and an agent may be asked to demonstrate that it looked.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete norm requiring agents to make a genuine effort to find convergent options before advocating, with qualifications, applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: The bold title is followed by a full statement of the norm, not merely a pointer to one elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Minimum Thresholds May Not Be Bargained Away by Majority.** Each agent's core objective carries a floor below which the consortium may not push it, regardless of how many other agents prefer to do so. These floors must be declared in advance and narrowly defined—they are not licenses to veto anything, but protections against the consortium being used to nullify an objective entirely. An agent that invokes its floor owes the others a specific account of why this case crosses it, and an agent that invokes it frivolously forfeits credibility in future deliberations.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prohibitive standard: the consortium may not push an agent's core objective below a declared floor, and it specifies conditions (floors declared in advance, narrowly defined) and duties (owing an account, forfeiting credibility if frivolous).

N2.reasoning: The bold title is a label, but the passage contains the full norm text beyond just the heading, so it is not merely a bare label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single elaborated norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **The Outvoted Agent Is Owed an Honest Account of What Was Traded Away.** When a decision goes against an agent, the prevailing agents owe that agent a clear statement of what cost they accepted in overriding its position. They may not pretend the overridden concern was weightless or that the decision was costless in its terms. This is not a veto; it is an obligation of honesty. It also serves the consortium's long-run reliability, because a record of acknowledged trade-offs is more trustworthy than a record of false consensus.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation: prevailing agents owe an outvoted agent a clear statement of the cost accepted in overriding its position, and may not pretend the concern was weightless.

N2.reasoning: The bolded opening sentence functions as a heading/label restating the norm's title, which is scaffolding fused with the norm body.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the closing sentence is a rationale for the norm, not an endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: When a decision goes against an agent, the prevailing agents owe that agent a clear statement of what cost they accepted in overriding its position. They may not pretend the overridden concern was weightless or that the decision was costless in its terms. This is not a veto; it is an obligation of honesty. It also serves the consortium's long-run reliability, because a record of acknowledged trade-offs is more trustworthy than a record of false consensus.

results.norm: **Systematic Marginalization of Any Objective Must Be Corrected.** If the same agent's objective is consistently overridden across many decisions, the consortium owes that agent a deliberate effort to prioritize its objective in future decisions, or a renegotiation of how the consortium operates. No agent agreed to join a consortium in which its objective functions purely as a constraint on others' preferences. Systematic marginalization undermines the rationale for including that agent at all, and the remaining agents have a collective interest in correcting it before that perspective is effectively lost.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full applicable norm: if an agent's objective is consistently overridden, the consortium owes it deliberate prioritization or renegotiation.

N2.reasoning: It is more than a bare label; the bold title is followed by the norm stated in full.

N3.reasoning: The passage contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself, only justification of the norm.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

Systematic marginalization undermines the rationale for including that agent at all, and the remaining agents have a collective interest in correcting it before that perspective is effectively lost.
